title: Expanding archive files without controlling resource consumption is security-sensitive
description: Expanding archive files without controlling resource consumption is security-sensitiveAnalyze your codeintentionality-completesecuritySecurity HotspotcwecertSuccessful Zip Bomb attacks occur when an application expands untrusted archive files without controlling the size of the expanded data, which can lead to denial of service. A Zip bomb is usually a malicious archive file of a few kilobytes of compressed data but turned into gigabytes of uncompressed data. To achieve this extremecompression ratio, attackers will compress irrelevant data (eg: a long string of repeated bytes).Ask Yourself WhetherArchives to expand are untrusted and:There is no validation of the number of entries in the archive.There is no validation of the total size of the uncompressed data.There is no validation of the
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Expanding archive files without controlling resource consumption is security-sensitive - implement size and count limits
